name: Mathematics
description: Mathematics provides the formal tools needed for rigorous AI safety research — from proving properties of systems to modeling risks.

people.name: Scott Aaronson
people.description: Theoretical computer scientist working on AI safety at OpenAI, bringing rigorous mathematical thinking to alignment problems.

people.name: Vanessa Kosoy
people.description: Develops mathematical frameworks for AI alignment, working on formal theories of embedded agency and learning theory.

people.name: John Wentworth
people.description: Works on mathematical foundations of alignment, particularly natural abstraction and agent foundations using category theory and topology.

trajectory: Proof-writing and formal verification skills are essential for developing AI systems with guaranteed safety properties.
Probability theory and statistics underpin uncertainty quantification and Bayesian approaches to AI safety.
Topology, category theory, and abstract algebra increasingly appear in cutting-edge alignment theory research.
Optimization theory helps understand and prevent reward hacking and specification gaming in AI systems.

projects.title: Formalize an alignment proposal
projects.description: Take an informal AI safety idea from a blog post or paper and develop a rigorous mathematical formalization, identifying hidden assumptions.

projects.title: Mathematical models of deceptive alignment
projects.description: Develop formal models of when and why an AI system might learn to appear aligned during training while pursuing different objectives.

projects.title: Contribute to formal verification for ML
projects.description: Work on mathematical methods for proving properties of neural networks, such as robustness bounds or behavioral guarantees.
